steps.title: Connect costs to donor size
steps.content: The passage says charities that compensate donors for nuisance costs will likely prefer a few large donors over many small donors. That only follows if the nuisance cost does not rise much with the donation amount.

steps.title: Reject the wrong relationships
steps.content: A says the costs are added to donation size, B says the costs predict donation size, and D says the costs are included within donation size. The remaining phrase means the costs are largely separate from donation size. The answer is C.
